zh.lifeSummary: Aubrey de Vere I於1030年12月16日出生在法國諾門迪曼其維。他於1061年與Beatrice de Ghent在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1107年被列為sheriff of berkshire。他去世於1112年12月26日在英國艾可享年82歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可易阿爾斯 可爾內。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, mostly from Vair in Ancenis, named with Gaulish ver(n)- ‘alder’. This was the probable home in the 11th century of the Breton lord Aubrey (Albericus) de Ver, who followed William the Conqueror to England and founded an influential family who held the earldom of Oxford until 1703. In a few cases the surname could be from other places in France, such as Ver in Manche and Calvados, which have the same etymology as Vair.
French (mainy Vendée; Veré): variant of Vairé, from Old French vairie ‘variegated, spotted’, applied as a nickname for someone who has freckles.

nameMeanings.name: Aubrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French form of the Germanic name Alberic, from alb ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ric ‘power’. This was the name, according to Germanic mythology, of the king of the elves. The native Old English form, Ælfrīc, borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury, did not long survive the Conquest. Aubrey was a relatively common given name during the Middle Ages, but later fell out of favour. Its occurrence since the 19th century may in part represent a transferred use of the surname derived from the Norman given name, as well as a revival of the latter. In the United States, this is mainly used as a girl's name, perhaps under the influence of Audrey .

variants.name: Aubree
meanings: From a Norman French form of the Germanic name Alberic, from alb ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ric ‘power’. This was the name, according to Germanic mythology, of the king of the elves. The native Old English form, Ælfrīc, borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury, did not long survive the Conquest. Aubrey was a relatively common given name during the Middle Ages, but later fell out of favour. Its occurrence since the 19th century may in part represent a transferred use of the surname derived from the Norman given name, as well as a revival of the latter. In the United States, this is mainly used as a girl's name, perhaps under the influence of Audrey .

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Aubrey de Vere I was born on 16 December 1030, in Ver, Manche, Normandy, France. He married Beatrice de Ghent in 1061, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. In 1107, his occupation is listed as sheriff of berkshire. He died on 26 December 1112, in Essex, England, at the age of 82, and was buried in Earls Colne, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Aubrey de Vere I est né le 16 décembre 1030, à Ver, Manche, Normandie, France. Il a épousé Beatrice de Ghent en 1061, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. En 1107, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of berkshire. Il est décédé le 26 décembre 1112, à Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterré à Earls Colne, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1107 ist sein Beruf als sheriff of berkshire aufgeführt. Er starb am 26. Dezember 1112 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Essex, England und wurde in Earls Colne, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Aubrey de Vere Iは1030年12月16日にフランスノーマンディマンチェバーで生まれた。1061年にイギリスエセックスでBeatrice de Ghentと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1107年，職業はsheriff of berkshireと記録されている。彼は1112年12月26日にイギリスエセックスで82歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスエアルルズ コルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Aubrey de Vere I(은)는 1030년 12월 16일 프랑스 노먼디 먼체 베르에서 태어났다. 그는 1061년 영국 에세스에서 Beatrice de Ghent(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1107년, 그의 직업은 sheriff of berkshire이었다. 그는 1112년 12월 26일 영국 에세스에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 에를스 코니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Aubrey de Vere I nasceu em 16 de dezembro de 1030, em Ver, Manche, Normandie, França. Ele casou-se com Beatrice de Ghent em 1061, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1107, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of berkshire. Ele faleceu em 26 de dezembro de 1112, em Essex, Inglaterra, com 82 anos, e foi sepultado em Earls Colne, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Aubrey de Vere I родился 16 декабря 1030 г. в местности вир, манч, норманди, Франция. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice de Ghent в 1061 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. В 1107 году он работал по профессии sheriff of berkshire. Он умер 26 декабря 1112 г. в местности эссекс, Англия в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности эарлс колн, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Aubrey de Vere I nació el 16 de diciembre de 1030, en Ver, Manche, Normandie, Francia. Se casó con Beatrice de Ghent en 1061, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. En 1107, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of berkshire. Murió el 26 de diciembre de 1112, en Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultado en Earls Colne, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
